stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 2 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability between 72% and 84% (uncertain due to limited operational data). satellite link 2 has a reliability of 92%. What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.9776 to 0.9872. Point estimate: 0.9824.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 satellite links fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.28 × 0.08 = 0.0224. P(works) = 0.9776.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.16 × 0.08 = 0.0128. P(works) = 0.9872.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
